title: quercus × autumnalis
intro: Quercus × autumnalis, also known as the white oak, is a deciduous tree that is a hybrid of the American white oak (Quercus alba) and the Japanese evergreen oak (Quercus acutissima). The bark, leaves, and acorns of this tree have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus × autumnalis include reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving immune function, and protecting against certain types of cancer. Quercus × autumnalis can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or extract, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall health and well-being.
